codeReview.summary: The follow-up README commit resolves the prior blocking problems. `README.md` now separates the compiler pipeline from validation, correctly states that parsing produces the AST, and adds the requested validation section with the flake-backed commands from `.factory/services.yaml` plus the downstream generated-Go acceptance checks that are exercised by `test/test_codegen.ml` and described in `validation-contract.md`. I did not find any new high-confidence issues in this re-review.

sharedStateObservations.observation: This docs-only feature is still assigned the `compiler-worker` skill even though that skill's required procedure is written for code changes and test-first implementation. The fix handoff again had to note that test creation was not applicable, which suggests the skill guidance should explicitly exempt documentation-only features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/761991a0-6428-436f-b034-0393187de1c7/features.json:2361-2363` assigns `final-readme-refresh` to `compiler-worker`; `/home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang/.factory/skills/compiler-worker/SKILL.md:22` requires workers to 'Write or extend tests first'; `/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/761991a0-6428-436f-b034-0393187de1c7/handoffs/2026-04-20T19-02-20-930Z__final-readme-refresh__cbadbe52-df93-4f0b-ad40-a17400297d50.json:34-41` says no test changes were needed because this is documentation-only and suggests adding a note that the test-first step does not apply.

summary: Review passed: commit `9a53383` fixes the prior failure by correcting the parsing wording and documenting the requested final validation/acceptance workflow in `README.md`, with the documented checks matching `.factory/services.yaml`, `validation-contract.md`, and `test/test_codegen.ml`.
